title: nasa humboldtiana subsp. humboldtiana
intro: Nasa humboldtiana subsp. humboldtiana, also known as the Humboldt's Nasa or Yellow Nasa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loasaceae. It is a native plant of the Andes mountain range, specifically found in countries such as Peru and Ecuador. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The leaves of the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function.
